Eagle Point Credit Company reported that management’s unaudited estimate of net asset value per share of its common stock as of March 31, 2026, was in a range of $4.12 to $4.22, reflecting the company’s latest view of its underlying portfolio value. For the quarter ended March 31, 2026, management estimated net investment income between $0.18 and $0.22 per share, with realized losses between $0.08 and $0.04 per share and additional realized losses from foreign currency contracts between $0.05 and $0.01 per share, indicating pressure on results from realized and currency-related losses despite ongoing income generation.

data.title: CaliberCos Launches Note Conversion and Preferred Stock Alignment
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from CaliberCos, Inc. Class A ( (CWD) ) is now available.

In March 2026, CaliberCos Inc. launched a note conversion program allowing holders of certain unsecured promissory notes to convert their debt into Class A common stock or newly created Series AAA Convertible Preferred Stock. By April 9, 2026, noteholders had converted and cancelled about $3.45 million of indebtedness into 1,707,900 Class A shares and 1,529 Series AAA preferred shares, issued as restricted securities in exempt transactions under U.S. securities laws.

On April 9, 2026, the company also aligned the rights of its existing Series A Convertible Preferred Stock with the new Series AAA class, including matching conversion terms and ranking the two series pari passu in liquidation preference. The detailed terms of both series provide 12% non-cumulative dividends, tiered conversion rates into Class A shares, senior ranking over common stock, and company redemption rights, underscoring an effort to restructure its capital stack and incentivize debt-to-equity conversion while preserving flexibility for future financing.

Spark’s Take on CWD Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CWD is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (sharp revenue decline, large losses, and significant 2025 cash burn) and bearish technicals (trading below key moving averages with negative MACD). Partially offsetting this are improving balance-sheet actions and constructive strategic updates from the earnings call, plus generally positive corporate developments, but these are not yet reflected in sustained profitability or cash generation.

To see Spark’s full report on CWD stock, click here.

Its securities are focused on providing structured equity offerings to investors, with its Class A common stock listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the ticker symbol CWD.

data.title: Black Hawk Acquisition Delays Quarterly SEC Filing
data.description:
Black Hawk Acquisition Corporation Class A ( (BKHA) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Black Hawk Acquisition Corporation has filed a Form 12b-25, notifying investors of a late filing for its Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) for the quarter ended February 28, 2026. The company, a smaller reporting issuer based in Danville, California, is seeking a short extension under SEC rules.

The delay stems from additional time needed to complete the financial statements included in the Form 10-Q (Quarter Report). Management states that filing on time would have required unreasonable effort or expense, but does not cite auditor changes, control problems, or special committee reviews.

The company expects to submit the Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) no later than the fifth calendar day after the original due date of April 14, 2026. That timeline is consistent with the standard grace period the SEC allows for late quarterly filings when a Form 12b-25 is used.

Black Hawk Acquisition Corporation does not anticipate any significant change in its results of operations versus the same quarter last year. No preliminary numbers are provided, and the notification contains forward-looking statements about timing that could be affected by future developments.

The company affirms that all other required periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed. The notification is signed by Chief Executive Officer Kent Louis Kaufman on April 14, 2026, underscoring management’s commitment to remain current with SEC reporting requirements despite the brief delay.

More about Black Hawk Acquisition Corporation Class A

data.title: Tianci International Signs MOU for Zimbabwe Mining Expansion
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Tianci International ( (CIIT) ) has provided an announcement.

On April 14, 2026, Tianci International announced it had signed a non-binding strategic cooperation Memorandum of Understanding with Zimbabwe-based Greypole Mineral Resources to pursue a future formal partnership. The contemplated collaboration aims to combine Tianci’s capital markets and logistics expertise with Greypole’s local mining capabilities to develop gold and chromium resources in Zimbabwe, marking a notable step in Tianci’s expansion into mineral resource development.

Under the MOU, the parties plan to jointly explore and extract gold across about 500 hectares in the Gwanda region and chromium across about 1,500 hectares in the Zvishavane region, while Greypole will lead compliant applications for exploration and mining rights under a phased implementation approach. Tianci’s chairman said the initiative is intended to turn Zimbabwe’s high-quality mineral reserves into tangible output and position the group to capture development opportunities in the sector, potentially deepening its presence in the global minerals supply chain and diversifying its revenue base beyond logistics.

Spark’s Take on CIIT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CIIT is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance—large operating losses and significant negative free cash flow—despite strong revenue growth and low leverage. Technicals add downside risk, with the stock in a pronounced downtrend and bearish momentum (only partly offset by oversold readings). Valuation is difficult to support due to negative earnings and no indicated dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on CIIT stock, click here.

More about Tianci International

Tianci International, Inc., listed on Nasdaq as CIIT, is a global logistics service provider that operates through its subsidiary Roshing, specializing in ocean freight forwarding for container and bulk goods. The company follows an asset-light model and serves customers across the Asia-Pacific region, while also engaging in global trade of high-grade minerals, along with sales of electronic parts and business consulting services.

Roshing’s logistics solutions are tailored to meet diverse customer needs in markets such as Japan, South Korea, and Vietnam, with a focus on efficient, reliable, and safe shipping that creates value for clients. Beginning in the current fiscal year, Tianci has expanded beyond logistics into sourcing minerals directly from resource-rich regions for resale, signaling a broader strategic push into commodity-based trading alongside its traditional services.

Average Trading Volume: 334,075

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $5.5M

Uniti Group ( (UNIT) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On April 13, 2026, Uniti Group Inc. filed a Certificate of Amendment to its Second Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation with the Delaware Secretary of State. The amendment changes the notification deadline for holders electing the method of payment for dividends on its Series A Preferred Stock from the first day of each dividend period to the 10th calendar day immediately preceding the relevant dividend payment date, affecting how and when preferred shareholders must make their dividend election.

This adjustment streamlines the timing of dividend payment elections for Series A Preferred Stock and may provide the company with clearer visibility into payment preferences closer to each payout date. The change could impact administrative processes and planning for both the company and its preferred shareholders, aligning dividend election logistics more tightly with actual payment schedules.

An update from Advanced Biomed Inc. ( (ADVB) ) is now available.

On April 13, 2026, Advanced Biomed Inc. entered into a loan agreement with lender Jie Wang for an unsecured U.S.$600,000 facility bearing 10% annual interest, with a six-month term from the disbursement date and an option to extend for an additional six months by mutual written consent. The company can prepay the loan without penalty, must repay principal plus accrued interest by maturity, and plans to use the proceeds to fund its operations, underscoring its near-term financing needs and reliance on short-term borrowing to support business activities.

The loan is governed by New York law, with any disputes to be resolved in New York courts, providing a standard legal framework for enforcement and signaling a conventional structure for this insider-style financing arrangement. For stakeholders, the transaction reflects a tactical move to shore up working capital without pledging collateral, balancing liquidity support against higher borrowing costs and refinancing risk at maturity.

More about Advanced Biomed Inc.

At its annual and special meeting of shareholders held on April 14, 2026, The Bank of Nova Scotia reported strong shareholder support for all management-backed items, including the election of 12 directors, the reappointment of KPMG LLP as auditor, amendments to By-law No. 1 on directors’ compensation and administrative matters, and an advisory resolution endorsing the bank’s executive compensation approach. Multiple shareholder proposals were presented but all were decisively rejected, with votes against ranging from roughly 78% to over 98%, underscoring broad investor alignment with the bank’s current governance, compensation practices, and strategic oversight.

The voting outcomes reinforce the stability of Scotiabank’s board and governance framework at a time when large financial institutions are under heightened scrutiny from regulators and investors. High approval rates on directors, pay practices, and by-law changes signal continued confidence from major shareholders, while the defeat of all shareholder proposals suggests limited momentum for activist-driven changes in the bank’s policies or strategic direction in the near term.

data.title: Otter Tail Announces Executive Leadership and CFO Succession
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Otter Tail ( (OTTR) ) has provided an update.

On April 13, 2026, Otter Tail Corporation’s board implemented a series of executive transitions as part of a long‑term succession plan. Tim Rogelstad, a 37‑year veteran who has led Otter Tail Power Company since 2014, was elected President of Otter Tail Corporation, while CEO Charles “Chuck” MacFarlane retained his role, and Rogelstad’s compensation was reset at a higher level aligned with other top executives.

Concurrently on April 13, 2026, long‑time finance executive Todd Wahlund stepped down as Chief Financial Officer to become Senior Vice President and President of Otter Tail Power Company, and Tyler Nelson was promoted to Vice President and Chief Financial Officer after advancing through key finance and accounting roles since joining the company in 2020. Both Wahlund and Nelson received updated compensation and equity‑based incentives tied to continued employment and change‑in‑control provisions, and Senior Vice President and General Counsel Jennifer Smestad was granted a $500,000 restricted stock retention award vesting in 2031, underscoring the company’s effort to stabilize and retain its leadership bench for long‑term execution.

The most recent analyst rating on (OTTR) stock is a Buy with a $83.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Otter Tail stock, see the OTTR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on OTTR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, OTTR is a Outperform.

OTTR scores well on solid profitability and an improving leverage profile, supported by a constructive technical trend and reasonable valuation. The score is held back by inconsistent free cash flow, 2025 margin compression, and earnings-call guidance pointing to near-term EPS pressure (notably Plastics) plus execution/timing risks.

To see Spark’s full report on OTTR stock, click here.

More about Otter Tail

Otter Tail Corporation is a diversified company with interests in an electric utility and manufacturing businesses, and is a member of the S&P SmallCap 600 Index. Its shares trade on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker OTTR, and the company is headquartered in Fergus Falls, Minnesota, and Fargo, North Dakota.

The corporation’s electric utility arm, Otter Tail Power Company, and its manufacturing operations form the core of its business platforms. These operations position Otter Tail as a regional utility and industrial player with a focus on long-term strategic execution and disciplined financial management.

The latest update is out from i-80 Gold Corp ( (TSE:IAU) ).

i-80 Gold Corp. reported on April 8, 2026, that infill drilling at the Upper Archimedes zone on its Ruby Hill property returned high-grade gold intercepts, including 24.6 grams per tonne over 23.6 meters, confirming continuity of mineralization and supporting resource expansion potential. The company has completed about 7,500 meters of drilling, is advancing underground development on schedule with roughly 1,200 meters completed, and is evaluating newly intersected oxide mineralization and a major Lower Archimedes drill campaign to underpin a new resource estimate and feasibility study, aligning with a staged plan targeting first gold from Upper Archimedes in the third quarter of 2026 and a projected 10-year mine life averaging 100,000 ounces of gold annually after ramp-up.

Ryanair Holdings ( (RYAAY) ) has shared an update.

Ryanair Holdings said that as of its financial year end on 31 March 2026, 30% of its issued share capital was held by EU nationals, including citizens of the EU-27, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway and Switzerland. This confirmation follows an earlier update in March 2025 on EU ownership levels.

The airline reiterated that, due to measures introduced on 1 January 2021 to comply with EU ownership and control rules for its EU airline subsidiaries, EU nationals are entitled to exercise 100% of the voting rights in Ryanair Holdings. The disclosure underscores Ryanair’s ongoing efforts to safeguard regulatory compliance and operational continuity for its European airlines in the post-Brexit environment, with implications for shareholding structure and voting power among investors.

data.title: Galera Announces Obsidian Merger and $350M Financing
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Galera Therapeutics ( (GRTX) ) just unveiled an update.

On April 14, 2026, Galera Therapeutics and privately held Obsidian Therapeutics announced a definitive all-stock merger agreement under which both companies will become subsidiaries of a newly formed parent that will operate as Obsidian Therapeutics, Inc. and seek a Nasdaq listing under the ticker “OBX.” At closing, pre-merger Obsidian holders are expected to own about 53.2% of the combined company, pre-merger Galera shareholders about 1.8%, and investors in a concurrent private placement about 45.0%, with Galera’s stake adjustable based on its net cash.

The deal is backed by an oversubscribed $350 million concurrent private placement expected to close immediately before the merger and to fund the combined company into the second half of 2028, including through key OBX-115 data readouts in NSCLC in the first half of 2027 and melanoma by year-end 2027. Existing Galera shareholders will also receive contingent value rights tied to future proceeds from legacy assets, while support and lock-up agreements, board approvals at both companies, and customary closing conditions position the transaction to close by the third quarter of 2026, reshaping Galera as a minority holder in an immuno-oncology-focused platform centered on Obsidian’s engineered TIL pipeline.

Spark’s Take on GRTX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GRTX is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (no revenue, ongoing operating losses, and limited evidence of sustainable cash generation), despite improved balance-sheet positioning in 2025. Technicals are relatively supportive with an uptrend and positive MACD, while valuation signals are unreliable due to likely non-operating earnings. The corporate event modestly helps by improving capital-structure flexibility but carries dilution risk.

To see Spark’s full report on GRTX stock, click here.

More about Galera Therapeutics

Galera Therapeutics, Inc. is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on advancing a pan-NOS inhibitor for patients with the hardest-to-treat forms of advanced breast cancer. The company was previously centered on developing avasopasem, a small molecule dismutase mimetic aimed at reducing chemoradiotherapy toxicities in head and neck cancer patients.

Obsidian Therapeutics, Inc., based in Cambridge, MA, is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company leveraging its cytoDRiVE protein-regulation platform to develop engineered tumor infiltrating lymphocyte (TIL) cell therapies for solid tumors. Its lead candidate, OBX-115, is in Phase 2 trials for advanced melanoma and Phase 1 trials for non-small cell lung cancer and has received Fast Track and RMAT designations in melanoma.

On April 14, 2026, National Bankshares released an investor presentation highlighting its fourth quarter 2025 performance and strategic progress, reporting assets of $1.8 billion, gross loans of $1.0 billion, deposits of $1.6 billion and GAAP net income of $5.9 million for the period ended December 31, 2025. The bank emphasized solid core profitability metrics, a 4.0% dividend yield based on 2025 payouts, and a recently upgraded core banking system designed to enhance technology for customers and internal operations.

The presentation underscored an inflection in net interest margin and profitability, driven by favorable asset and liability repricing and a strong liquidity position that has helped manage deposit costs. Management also detailed growth initiatives stemming from the 2024 acquisition of Frontier Community Bank and new branches in Roanoke and Lynchburg, as well as targeted hiring and promotions in high‑growth markets such as Waynesboro, Staunton and the Roanoke Valley, positioning the company to deploy excess liquidity and expand in economically dynamic regions.

National Bankshares also highlighted the stabilizing impact of diversified fee income, including steadily growing trust revenues, alongside disciplined expense management that has improved its efficiency ratio. These developments collectively signal a strategy aimed at sustaining improved earnings, strengthening competitive standing in its Virginia footprint and delivering long‑term value for shareholders through both operational upgrades and geographic expansion.

Mint Incorporation Limited Class A ( (MIMI) ) has provided an announcement.

On April 14, 2026, Mint Incorporation Limited announced that its wholly owned subsidiary Axonex Intelligence was exhibiting at InnoEX in Hong Kong from April 13–16, 2026, underscoring Mint’s strategic pivot toward AI and robotics alongside its established fit-out business. The company used the high-profile regional technology fair to highlight its growing robotics portfolio and signal its ambition to build a broader smart-industry ecosystem rooted in Hong Kong and expanded into overseas markets.

At the show, Axonex presented six robot series, led by the production-oriented semi-humanoid robot NEX, making its Hong Kong debut with NVIDIA-powered embodied AI, proprietary databases, and advanced vision and grasping capabilities for medical, industrial, retail, and exhibition uses. It also unveiled companion and patrol robots and a suite of AI platforms spanning home, security, workplace, aerial, and lift-control applications, positioning Mint to address labour costs, operational efficiency, and smart-building needs across Asia and beyond.

More about Mint Incorporation Limited Class A

Mint Incorporation Limited is a Hong Kong-based enterprise listed on Nasdaq that focuses on artificial intelligence, robotics, and interior design. Through subsidiaries Axonex Intelligence and Matter International, it provides intelligent robotics and facility-management solutions as well as professional interior design and renovation services to enterprises, real estate, shopping centres, government agencies, and other clients.

data.title: Perma-Pipe Establishes New $18 Million Credit Facility
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Perma-Pipe International Holdings ( (PPIH) ) just unveiled an update.

On April 8, 2026, Perma-Pipe International Holdings, Inc. entered into a senior secured asset-based revolving credit facility with JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. totaling $18.0 million, including a $1.5 million sublimit for letters of credit, maturing on October 7, 2027. The facility is governed by a borrowing base tied to eligible receivables and inventory, carries interest based on either a CB Floating Rate or adjusted term SOFR plus a leverage-linked margin, and includes commitment fees on unused capacity.

The obligations are secured by substantially all assets of the company and certain guarantor subsidiaries, with guarantees provided on a joint and several basis by designated current and future subsidiaries, subject to typical exceptions. The agreement imposes customary covenants limiting additional debt, liens, investments, acquisitions, asset sales, restricted payments, and affiliate transactions, and it features a minimum fixed charge coverage ratio test and standard events of default that allow the lender to accelerate obligations and enforce remedies, influencing the company’s financial flexibility and liquidity management framework.

The most recent analyst rating on (PPIH) stock is a Buy with a $29.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Perma-Pipe International Holdings stock, see the PPIH Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PPIH is a Outperform.

Perma-Pipe International Holdings demonstrates strong financial performance with robust revenue growth and improved cash flow, which are the most significant factors in its overall score. The technical analysis indicates a strong upward trend, though caution is advised due to overbought signals. The valuation is reasonable, supporting the company’s growth prospects. The recent board appointment is a positive but minor factor in the overall assessment.

Perma-Pipe International Holdings, Inc. operates in the industrial and infrastructure sector, providing engineered piping systems and related solutions used in energy, industrial, and district heating and cooling applications. The company serves customers that require specialized, often large-scale piping infrastructure, positioning it as a niche provider within the broader construction and energy supply chain markets.
